title: debrief corpus (hot file)
description: The portable, evidence-free hot file for the debrief developer-context corpus. Carries patterns (clusters), accumulated answers, the alias index, standing-protocol state, and ingestion sources. Raw evidence snippets live ONLY in the separate evidence sidecar (corpus.evidence.json), never here.

schemaVersion.description: Hot-file schema version. Bumped on any breaking shape change.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last write.

sources.description: Ingestion sources. v1 populates claude-sessions / claude-memory; a 'git' slot is reserved but unpopulated.

clusters.description: The mined patterns, each identified by an opaque surrogate clusterId.

aliases.description: Lookup index: composite (detector, normalizedSubject) key -> clusterId. NOT the identity; answers hang off clusterId so a remap never orphans them. The key joins detector and normalizedSubject with a NUL (U+0000) byte.

protocols.description: Standing-protocol hypotheses about how the developer works.

themes.description: Tier 2 — broad themes: a non-destructive overlay grouping related clusters under abstract, question-able themes. Evidence-free (memberClusterIds + answers only).

AnswerSource.description: Provenance of an answer. 'user' (human-confirmed ground truth) outranks 'inferred' (agent-reasoned).

Kind.description: R/O/C/Q/X intent taxonomy: R=redirect, O=observed, C=continue, Q=query, X=not-a-real-turn. The connected agent's classification, never the CLI's.

text.description: Answer text. Part of the portable surface — the caller is responsible for keeping it snippet-free.

clusterId.description: Opaque surrogate UUID. The answer-bearing identity. Minted once, never derived from content.

detector.description: Structural candidate-source label (e.g. 'after-error', 'turn-after-completion'). NOT a lexical/intent matcher.

normalizedSubject.description: Coarse deterministic CLI-derived subject. The current index key; may be re-keyed via aliases.

summary.description: Short human-readable description of the behavior.

primaryKind.description: Primary R/O/C/Q/X kind (the connected agent's classification). Optional — absent until tagged via set_cluster_kind.

secondaryKind.description: Optional secondary R/O/C/Q/X kind, set only when the turn genuinely does two things.

count.description: Total occurrences (distinct evidence) across all sessions. Volatile; grows on re-extraction.

sessionCount.description: Number of distinct sessions the behavior appeared in. Volatile.

evidenceIds.description: Keys into the evidence sidecar. Snippets themselves live there, NOT here.

question.description: Optional cached question text. Normally ABSENT — questions are regenerated by the connected LLM.

merged.description: True iff this cluster is the target of an agent-driven semantic merge (merge_clusters) — it absorbed another cluster's aliases + evidence + answers. Absent on raw (CLI-coarse) clusters.

pending.description: Pending-question state. Present iff a question was forwarded to the user (answer_open_question mode:'user') and not yet resolved by a confirmed source:user answer. Re-surfaces across sessions; never expires; cleared when the user answers. Absent on clusters with no outstanding forwarded question.

firstSeen.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the cluster was first created. Stable — set once, never changed (a merge keeps the earlier of the two). The deterministic oldest-first tiebreak for surfacing order. Optional for backward-compat.

lastActivityAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last activity touching this cluster (creation, new evidence merged, or a merge). Volatile. Optional for backward-compat.

relational.description: Objective relational FACTS (T7): COUNTS + TIMESTAMPS recomputed from the cluster's sidecar evidence on every add/merge/split. PRIVACY-CLEAN (no raw paths) and VERDICT-FREE (the agent interprets them). Optional for backward-compat.

RelationalFacts.description: Objective relational FACTS for a cluster (T7). FACTS ONLY — COUNTS + TIMESTAMPS, no verdict/label/threshold. PRIVACY-CLEAN: distinctRepos/distinctBranches are COUNTS of distinct cwd/gitBranch values; the raw paths/branches are NEVER copied to the hot file.

distinctSessions.description: Number of distinct sessions the cluster's evidence spans.

distinctRepos.description: Number of DISTINCT cwd values across the cluster's sidecar evidence. A COUNT — raw paths never copied.

distinctBranches.description: Number of DISTINCT gitBranch values across the cluster's sidecar evidence. A COUNT — raw branches never copied.

firstTs.description: Earliest evidence timestamp (ISO-8601), if any evidence carried a ts.

lastTs.description: Latest evidence timestamp (ISO-8601), if any evidence carried a ts.

occurrences.description: Total occurrences = number of distinct evidence items in the cluster (mirrors count).

forwardedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the question was first forwarded to the user. The oldest-first sort key.

skipCount.description: Times this pending question has been skipped (surfaced but deferred). At or above the skip threshold (K) the question is demoted in surfacing priority.

lastSurfacedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp it was last skipped (via skip_question), if ever. Stamped only on skip; surfacing via get_pending_questions is a lock-free read that does not stamp it.

Theme.description: Tier 2 — a broad theme grouping related clusters non-destructively. Evidence-free: holds only memberClusterIds + answers (never snippets). Carries its own answers + pending (question-able at the abstract level).

themeId.description: Opaque surrogate UUID. The theme's answer-bearing identity.

memberClusterIds.description: The clusters grouped under this theme. A non-destructive reference set; a cluster may belong to multiple themes. Every id MUST reference an existing cluster.

answers.description: Accumulated theme-level answers (user outranks inferred at read time).

pending.description: Theme-level pending-question state (mirrors a cluster's). Present iff a theme question was forwarded to the user and not yet resolved.

firstSeen.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the theme was created. Stable; oldest-first surfacing tiebreak.

lastActivityAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last activity touching this theme (create, regroup, answer).
